name: fitai-coach
//overrides: Security overrides - npm uses 'overrides', pnpm uses 'pnpm.overrides'.
Both sections MUST be kept in sync. See pnpm.overrides near the bottom of this file.
esbuild 0.27.5 (exact pin): fixes GHSA-67mh-4wv8-2f99 (permissive CORS on esbuild dev server, fixed in 0.27.0).
Pulled by vite (depends on esbuild ^0.21.3). Still needed as of vite 5.4.21.
Pinned to ~0.27.5 (not ^0.27.0) because esbuild 0.27.7+ (through 0.28.0) regressed the
transform-check path: with vite's default `supported: { dynamic-import, import-meta }`
overrides, esbuild erroneously demands destructuring lowering for chrome87/es2020/
firefox78/safari14 targets and fails the client build with 'Transforming destructuring
... is not supported yet'. Re-evaluate bumping past 0.27.5 when upstream fixes the regression.
Remove when vite is upgraded to a version that depends on esbuild >=0.27.0.
yauzl ^3.2.1: fixes CVE-2026-31988 (DoS via off-by-one in NTFS timestamp parser).
Pulled by extract-zip ^2.0.1 (via cypress), which depends on yauzl ^2.10.0. Still needed.
Remove when cypress ships an extract-zip that depends on yauzl >=3.2.1.
undici ^7.24.3: fixes CVE-2026-1526/1527/1528/2229/2581 (memory exhaustion, CRLF injection, WebSocket crashes).
Pulled by jsdom (depends on undici ^7.21.0). The parent range now resolves to >=7.24.3
on fresh installs, so this override is SAFE TO REMOVE on next lockfile regeneration.
Kept for now as defense-in-depth. Re-evaluate after next pnpm install.
serialize-javascript >=7.0.3: fixes GHSA-5c6j-r48x-rmvq (RCE via unsanitized RegExp.flags).
Pulled by @rollup/plugin-terser (depends on serialize-javascript ^7.0.3).
REMOVABLE NOW: ^7.0.3 already satisfies >=7.0.3; the parent dep has been updated.
hono >=4.12.14: fixes three hono <4.12.14 vulnerabilities:
- CVE-2026-39410 / GHSA-r5rp-j6wh-rvv4 (getCookie NBSP prefix bypass, fixed in 4.12.12):
Hono's parse() used String.prototype.trim() on cookie names and stripped U+00A0,
collapsing '\u00a0dummy-cookie' onto 'dummy-cookie' and defeating __Secure-/__Host- prefixes.
- ipRestriction() IPv4-mapped IPv6 bypass (fixed in 4.12.12): IPv4 clients connecting over
dual-stack (::ffff:127.0.0.1) evaded IPv4 allow/deny rules because addresses weren't
canonicalized.
- GHSA-458j-xx4x-4375 (JSX attribute-name HTML injection, fixed in 4.12.14): hono/jsx SSR
inserted JSX attribute keys into output without validation; keys containing '"', '>', or
whitespace could break out of the attribute/tag and inject HTML when untrusted input was
used as attribute names.
Pulled by @modelcontextprotocol/sdk 1.25.2 (depends on hono 4.12.10) and referenced
by @google/genai via an optional peer. We don't import hono directly; this override is
defense-in-depth so MCP SDK code paths cannot inherit the vulnerable parser / matcher / renderer.
Remove when @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ships a version that depends on hono >=4.12.14.
@hono/node-server ^1.19.13: fixes GHSA (serveStatic middleware bypass via repeated slashes).
Path-handling inconsistency lets '//admin/secret.txt' bypass route-based middleware on
'/admin/*' and access protected static files. Patched in 1.19.13.
Pulled by @modelcontextprotocol/sdk 1.29.0 (depends on @hono/node-server 1.19.12).
This repo does not use Hono directly (Express-based), but we pin the patched version to
clear the advisory and reduce supply-chain exposure. Dependabot cannot update transitive
deps for pnpm, so the override is required.
Remove when @modelcontextprotocol/sdk ships a version that depends on @hono/node-server >=1.19.13.
protobufjs ^7.5.5: fixes GHSA (arbitrary code execution via crafted 'type'
fields in protobuf definitions; attacker-controlled descriptors inject JS
that runs during decode()). Pulled by @google/genai 1.50.1 (depends on
protobufjs 7.5.4). Constrained to the 7.x major line because @google/genai
declares compatibility with protobufjs 7.x; a bare >=7.5.5 resolves to 8.x
and risks API drift. We do not import protobufjs or @google/genai directly,
but we pin the patched version to clear the advisory and reduce
supply-chain exposure. Dependabot cannot update transitive deps for pnpm,
so the override is required.
Remove when @google/genai ships a version that depends on protobufjs >=7.5.5.
@clerk/shared ^4.8.1: fixes GHSA (createRouteMatcher / createPathMatcher middleware bypass).
Crafted requests could skip middleware-level route gating in @clerk/nextjs, @clerk/nuxt,
and @clerk/astro. Patched in @clerk/shared 4.8.1.
Pulled transitively by @clerk/react 6.1.4 (depends on @clerk/shared 4.4.0). @clerk/express
2.1.5 and @clerk/backend 3.2.13 already resolve to 4.8.2, so they are unaffected.
This repo does not use createRouteMatcher/createPathMatcher and is not functionally
exposed, but Dependabot cannot update transitive pnpm deps, so the override is required
to clear the advisory.
Remove when @clerk/react ships a version that depends on @clerk/shared >=4.8.1.
follow-redirects ^1.16.0: fixes custom auth header leak on cross-domain redirect
(Dependabot issue #35). In follow-redirects <=1.15.11, the cross-domain header
scrubber at index.js:469-476 only matches /^(?:(?:proxy-)?authorization|cookie)$/i,
so custom auth headers (X-API-Key, X-Auth-Token, Api-Key, etc.) are forwarded
verbatim to the redirect target. Patched in 1.16.0.
Pulled transitively by @flow-js/garmin-connect 1.6.16 -> axios -> follow-redirects.
Garmin is the only axios consumer in this repo (server/garmin.ts); all other
outbound HTTP uses native fetch. The Garmin SDK carries encrypted OAuth1/OAuth2
tokens and email/password credentials, so a malicious redirect from the Garmin
API hostname could exfiltrate them. Dependabot cannot update transitive pnpm
deps, so the override is required.
Remove when @flow-js/garmin-connect ships a release whose axios pulls follow-redirects >=1.16.0.

pnpm.//overrides: Mirror of top-level 'overrides' (for npm). Keep both in sync. See //overrides above for rationale.
